center.name: 徐畢克斯
center.summary: 徐畢克斯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联14种鸟类，包括白腹拟䳍、黄腿穴䳍、Scarlet-horned manakin等14种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-backed water tyrant (Fluvicola albiventer) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Paraguay, Peru, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Sclater]], 1862 | species = strigilatus | authority = (Spix, 1825) | synonyms = | range_map = Ancistrops strigilatus map.svg }} The chestnut-winged hookbill (Ancistrops strigilatus) is a species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The eastern woodhaunter (Automolus subulatus), also known as the Amazonian woodhaunter, is a species of bird in the Furnariinae subfamily of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela.Clements, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The masked tityra (Tityra semifasciata) is a medium-sized passerine bird. It is found in Mexico, every Central American country, and every northern and central mainland South American country except the Guianas.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The ocellated woodcreeper (Xiphorhynchus ocellatus) is a species of bird in the subfamily Dendrocolaptinae of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, and Venezuela.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The planalto woodcreeper (Dendrocolaptes platyrostris) is a sub-oscine passerine bird in subfamily Dendrocolaptinae of the ovenbird family Furnariidae. It is found in Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The rufous-tailed flatbill (Ramphotrigon ruficauda) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in every mainland South American country except Argentina, Chile, Paraguay, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The scarlet-horned manakin (Ceratopipra cornuta) is a species of bird in the family Pipridae. It is found in Brazil, Guyana, and Venezuela.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | status = LC | status_system = IUCN3.1 | status_ref = | genus = Myiozetetes | species = similis | authority = (Spix, 1825) | synonyms = Muscicapa similis (protonym) | range_map = Myiozetetes similis map.svg }} The social flycatcher (Myiozetetes similis) is a passerine bird from the Americas, a member of the large tyrant flycatcher family Tyrannidae. It is found in Mexico, every Central American country, and every mainland South American country except Chile, Guyana, Suriname, and Uruguay.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The spot-winged antshrike (Pygiptila stellaris) is a species of bird in subfamily Myrmornithinae of family Thamnophilidae, the "typical antbirds". It is found in Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Guyana, Peru, Suriname, Venezuela, and possibly French Guiana.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Song of white-shouldered antbird (Akletos melanoceps) The white-shouldered antbird (Akletos melanoceps) is a species of bird in subfamily Thamnophilinae of family Thamnophilidae, the "typical antbirds". It is found in Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, and Peru.Remsen, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: First described by Johann Baptist von Spix in 1825, this American sparrow is found across much of the Amazon basin in South America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The white-bellied nothura (Nothura boraquira) is a species of tinamou found in dry shrublands in northeastern Bolivia, western Paraguay, and northeastern Brazil

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: zabele (Spix, 1825) | range_map = Crypturellus noctivagus map.svg }} The yellow-legged tinamou (Crypturellus noctivagus) is a species of tinamou found in wooded and shrubby habitats in tropical and subtropical eastern Brazil. This superficially quail-like bird has a grey-brown plumage and two easily separated subspecies
